channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: EU, UK hit Russia with joint sanctions over cyber attacks
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The EU and UK impose joint sanctions on Russia, targeting FSB and GRU over persistent cyber attacks destabilizing Europe and critical infrastructure. Learn
Read More: https://punchng.com/eu-uk-hit-russia-with-joint-sanctions-over-cyber-attacks/

item.title: UN, FG seek innovative financing to fast-track SDGs before 2030
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The UN and Nigerian government call for innovative financing and stronger partnerships to accelerate the achievement of Sustainable Development Goals befor
Read More: https://punchng.com/un-fg-seek-innovative-financing-to-fast-track-sdgs-before-2030/

item.title: Sowore offers to help DJ Chicken get out of prison
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Omoyele Sowore offers to advocate for DJ Chicken, who was arraigned for allegedly threatening Seyi Tinubu. Sowore criticises the charges and seeks to help
Read More: https://punchng.com/sowore-offers-to-help-dj-chicken-get-out-of-prison/

item.title: VIDEO: Nigerians deserve full transparency, Makinde seeks UN probe into Oyo abduction
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Governor Makinde calls for a UN probe into the Oyo abduction, demanding full transparency and accountability for the incident involving pupils and teachers
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-nigerians-deserve-full-transparency-makinde-seeks-un-probe-into-oyo-abduction/

item.title: McGregor to undergo surgery but hopes to fight again
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Conor McGregor will undergo surgery after his return fight against Max Holloway at UFC 329 ended prematurely due to knee injury, but he hopes to fight again.

item.title: Ekiti approves N16.75bn for road, streetlight projects
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ekiti State Executive Council has approved N16.75bn for major road reconstruction and solar streetlight projects to boost infrastructure and development.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ekiti-approves-n16-75bn-for-road-streetlight-projects/

item.title: Fix fuel pricing system, not just pump price, don urges FG
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A petroleum economist urges the FG to fix Nigeria’s fuel pricing system, focusing on market efficiency and transparency rather than just pump prices.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fix-fuel-pricing-system-not-just-pump-price-don-urges-fg/

item.title: Why abducted Oyo teachers were killed by kidnappers — Rescued school principal
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Rescued principal Mrs. Rachael Alamu explains why kidnappers killed abducted Oyo teachers, Mr. Michael Oyedokun and Mr. Esiyan Adegboye, during their 56-da
Read More: https://punchng.com/why-abducted-oyo-teachers-were-killed-by-kidnappers-rescued-school-principal/

item.title: EFCC arraigns entertainment CEO over alleged N16.8m dud cheque
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The EFCC has arraigned Ski Hi-Entertainment CEO, Ifeanyichukwu Ogbu, in Enugu over alleged N16.8m fraud involving dud cheques. Get the full details here.
Read More: https://punchng.com/efcc-arraigns-entertainment-ceo-over-alleged-n16-8m-dud-cheque/

item.title: NMDPRA seals two Ogun filling stations over under-dispensing, sharp practices
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NMDPRA has sealed two Ogun filling stations for under-dispensing and other sharp practices, enforcing the Petroleum Industry Act 2021 to protect consum
Read More: https://punchng.com/nmdpra-seals-two-ogun-filling-stations-over-under-dispensing-sharp-practices/

item.title: Oyebanji retains governing councils of Ekiti tertiary institutions
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ekiti State Governor, Biodun Oyebanji, retains governing councils of all state-owned tertiary institutions, citing their performance in repositioning highe
Read More: https://punchng.com/oyebanji-retains-governing-councils-of-ekiti-tertiary-institutions/

item.title: ‘DJ Chicken does not deserve to be in prison’ – Sowore offers to help
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: African Action Congress (AAC) presidential candidate, Omoyele Sowore has offered to support controversial disc jockey and TikTok personality, Ademola Abiodun, popularly known as DJ Chicken, following his arraignment over alleged threats against Seyi Tinubu, son of President Bola Tinubu. The post &#8

item.title: HBM Nigeria changes NGX trading symbol from WAPCO to HBMNG
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: HBM Nigeria Plc has changed its trading symbol on the Nigerian Exchange (NGX) from WAPCO to HBMNG, completing another key step in its transition from Lafarge Africa Plc to its new corporate identity. The post HBM Nigeria changes NGX trading symbol from WAPCO to HBMNG appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Gov Kefas Inaugurates New Appointees, Assures Of Better Service Delivery
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: According to the governor, his administration will not tolerate indiscipline, truancy, or any act capable of jeopardising the government's desire to enhance good governance. The post Gov Kefas Inaugurates New Appointees, Assures Of Better Service Delivery appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Why kidnappers killed two Oyo teachers – Rescued Principal
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the principal, the kidnappers believed killing the teachers would pile pressure on the government to accede to their demands, having realised that the mass abduction had attracted national and international attention.

item.title: ‘2026 Hajj applicants to get priority consideration for 2027 pilgrimage’ — Bauchi Pilgrims Board
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Bauchi State Muslim Pilgrims Welfare Board has assured intending pilgrims who paid for the 2026 Hajj but were unable to travel to Saudi Arabia that they will receive priority consideration for the 2027 pilgrimage. The Executive Secretary of the board, Imam Abdurrahman Ibrahim Idris, gave the ass

item.title: Meta and Amazon are leading a trillion-dollar Big Tech spending spree
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: As the cost of AI infrastructure continues to increase, Morgan Stanley says Big Tech capital expenditures are on track to hit new records.

item.title: States make last-ditch effort to stop the Paramount ‘media behemoth’
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: A dozen state attorneys general are trying to block the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros Discovery they warn would raise movie prices and crush cable TV distributors. The states - California, Arizona, Colorado, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, Ne

item.title: Court of Appeal judgement does not affect our primaries – ADC
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “We wish to assure members of the party and the general public that this judgment has no effect whatsoever on the direct primaries through which the party’s candidates have emerged at all levels.” The post Court of Appeal judgement does not affect our primaries – ADC appeared first on Premium Times

item.title: Alonso: I Will Get The Best Out Of Palmer
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Chelsea manager Xabi Alonso has disclosed that Cole Palmer is a special talent, and he is working on building the team to get the absolute best out of him this season. During his introductory press conference, Alonso expressed immediate admiration for Palmer’s intelligence, decision-making, and spec

item.title: Micah Richards makes decision on quitting punditry
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Former Manchester City and England defender Micah Richards has revealed he plans to step away from television punditry within the next eight years, despite enjoying huge success since retiring from football. The post Micah Richards makes decision on quitting punditry appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: 𝗨𝗞 𝗚𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗔𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗰𝗲𝘀 £250m 𝗧𝗼 𝗕𝗼𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝗣𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗲 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 For 𝗝𝗲𝘄𝗶𝘀𝗵 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝘂𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗲𝘀
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Britain’s government on Monday announced more than £250 million to boost police protection of the Jewish community, which in recent months has been targeted by a series of attacks. The fresh package will help fund the deployment of more than 500 police officers in districts where a lot of British Je

item.title: My wife’s 2011 Ford Fiesta was totaled. Should we accept a $2,000 insurance settlement or give up the car for $2,700?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “The hood is bent, the radiator is cracked, and the front bumper is destroyed.”

item.title: ‘Court judgment will not affect our candidates, primary elections’ – ADC
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has assured its members and supporters that the recent Court of Appeal judgment on the party’s congresses will not affect its primary elections or the candidates who emerged from them. In a statement issued on Monday by the party’s National Publicity Secretary,

item.title: Mutual Fund Managers Missing 'Biblical' Opportunity: Smead
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Cole Smead, CEO of Smead Capital, joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." Smead outlines the benefits of mutual funds and how they can match ETF tax efficiency. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Era of internal sabotage over – lPOB declares
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Indigenous People of Biafra, IPOB, has declared that the era of internal sabotage in the organization is over.  The declaration followed the resolution of the recent internal crisis in the organization, which led to the expulsion of some leading figures.à The newly inaugurated Head of the 4

item.title: APM confident Makinde will defeat Tinubu in 2027 presidential election
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Allied People’s Movement (APM) has expressed confidence that its presidential candidate, Governor Seyi Makinde of Oyo State, will defeat President Bola Tinubu in the 2027 presidential election. The party made the declaration on Monday after confirming that it had submitted the nomination d

item.title: Group Urges Makinde To Name School After Teacher Killed In Oyo Attack
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Yoruba cultural advocate and Lead of Abinibi Hub, Bunmi Adetona, has appealed to Oyo State governor Seyi Makinde, to immortalise the late Michael Oyedokun, one of the teachers killed during the May 15 attack on schools in Oriire Local Government Area, by naming a secondary school after him. Adeton

item.title: Nigeria launches food procurement guidelines to tackle unhealthy diets, improve nutrition
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mr Salako noted that unhealthy diets contribute to about 7.2 million deaths globally every year. The post Nigeria launches food procurement guidelines to tackle unhealthy diets, improve nutrition appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Willing to pay: how much? Ukraine allies in Paris without the United States

item.description: Forget last week's NATO summit where Donald Trump put the alliance through an emotional rollercoaster. It's Bastille Day in Paris, with the so-called coalition of the willing - aka Nato minus the United States - serving up some more serene summitry on the eve of the traditional military parade down

item.title: Oil jumps 4% to $79 per barrel as US-Iran tensions escalate
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Obas Esiedesa, Abuja Global oil prices climbed by more than four per cent on Monday, with Brent crude rising to about $79 per barrel, as renewed hostilities between the United States and Iran heightened concerns over disruptions to global crude supplies. The price rally followed fresh military ex

item.title: Makinde seeks UN probe into abduction of Oyo pupils, teachers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Governor of Oyo State, Seyi Makinde, has called on the United Nations, UN, and international human rights organisations to investigate the abduction of schoolchildren and teachers in Oriire LGA on May 15. The governor made this known while speaking in a broadcast after receiving the rescued pupi

item.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

item.title: Graham’s Death Deprives Ukraine of Key Trump Whisperer
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The death of Senator Lindsey Graham leaves key US allies like Ukraine, which have at times struggled to influence the Trump administration, without one of their most effective conduits to the White House. Rebeccah Heinrichs, senior fellow at Hudson Institute, joined Balance of Power and said there's

item.title: Oyo abduction: We spent more than four hours on road – Rescued school principal [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Mrs Rachael Folawe Alamu, the rescued school principal of Community High School in Oriire Local Government Area LGA, of Oyo State has revealed that they spent at least four hours on the road when the terrorists were moving them into a tick forest. She stated this on Monday during the offic

item.title: Global oil prices top $82 a barrel, heading for biggest jump in 3 months after Trump reimposes Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The week is kicking off with fresh spikes for oil prices after President Donald Trump reimposed the naval blockage on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Kano Police Intercept 123 Vehicles, Recover Pistol, Cannabis In Number Plate Crackdown
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Kano State Police Command says it has intercepted 123 vehicles operating with covered, defaced or missing registration plates across the state, while recovering a Beretta pistol, suspected cannabis and other exhibits from a suspect arrested during the ongoing enforcement exercise. item.title: Nigerians will never forget Buhari’s service — Tinub
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Eminent Nigerians on Monday paid glowing tribute to late former President Muhammadu Buhari, on the occasion of his one-year remembrance held in Abuja. President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, who was Special Guest of Honour, described his late predecessor as a leader Nigerians are not capable of forgetting beca

item.title: NJ Takes Step Toward New Nuclear Plants as Sherrill Signs Bill
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: New Jersey is initiating a process to develop new nuclear power plants to meet rising demand for electricity.

item.title: Spanish Embassy Trains Nigerian Journalists To Tackle Electoral Disinformation Ahead Of 2027 Polls
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Spanish Embassy in Nigeria has reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening credible journalism and combating electoral disinformation by equipping Nigerian journalists with advanced fact-checking skills ahead of the 2027 general elections. The embassy, through its Cultural Office, organised a two

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: A surge in U.S. inflation to a three-year high has begun to recede, but life isn’t going to get more affordable for Americans anytime soon.

item.title: Leveraged ETFs Safer Than Some Options: Tuttle

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A group of Canadian oil sands producers reached an agreement with the Alberta and federal governments on next steps toward building a carbon capture and storage system.

item.title: The EV market is on the road to recovery thanks to high gas prices
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: As the US war on Iran sent gas prices soaring, American car buyers flocked to electric vehicles - and in particular hybrids. After a sluggish winter and fall, EV sales rebounded in the second quarter of 2026, with automakers posting some of their best numbers since the elimination of the federal EV

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Principal of Community High School, Ahoro-Esiele, Mrs. Alamu Folawe, has recounted the traumatic 56-day ordeal she and other abducted teachers and pupils endured in captivity. She revealed that the kidnappers deliberately killed two of their colleagues in a bid to pressure the government. Folawe

item.title: Diamond League: Ajayi set for London showdown ahead of Commonwealth Games
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Fresh from a sensational Diamond League debut victory in Eugene, Nigeria's sprint sensation Kayinsola Ajayi will face another stern test against some of the world's fastest men at Saturday's London Diamond League. The post Diamond League: Ajayi set for London showdown ahead of Commonwealth Games app

item.title: Oriire abduction: Makinde vows stronger security, seeks UN probe
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, has said the state government would strengthen security around communities bordering the Old Oyo National Park following the safe return of pupils and teachers abducted from Ahoro-Esinle and Yawota communities in Oriire Local Government Area. In a statewide broadcas

item.title: FG Eyes Safer Buildings With Fresh Regulatory Framework
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Minister of Housing and Urban Development, Engr. Muttaqha Rabe Darma, has declared that Nigeria’s built environment industry must be properly regulated to enhance safety, improve standards, and unlock the contribution of the sector to the nation’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP). The Minister made

item.title: Warsh’s Balance-Sheet Panel Runs Into Market Reality Check
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Wall Street strategists expect a group of senior academics and former policymakers assembled by Chairman Kevin Warsh to review the Federal Reserve’s $6.7 trillion balance sheet will face a difficult balancing act: shrinking the central bank’s holdings without destabilizing funding markets.

item.title: Our Abductors Killed Two Teachers To Pressure Government – Oriire School Principal
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Newly-freed school principal, Mrs Rachael Alamu, has revealed that the abductors killed two teachers to pressure the government into giving in to their demands. She revealed this on Monday as the Nigerian Army handed over the pupils and teachers rescued from kidnappers in Oriire Local Government Are

item.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

item.title: Police demote two officers over POS extortion scandal
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Police have demoted an inspector and a corporal after they were found guilty of extorting a motorist through a POS terminal inside a police station, reinforcing the Inspector-General's ban on electronic payments in police formations. The post Police demote two officers over POS extortion scandal app

item.title: RBC Expands Credit Derivatives Trading as AI Debt Fuels Hedging
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Royal Bank of Canada is expanding its credit derivatives trading business in the US and Europe, betting that multibillion-dollar fundraising for artificial intelligence will fuel demand for hedging products.

item.title: Appeal Court Judgment Won’t Affect ADC Primaries, Party Insists
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has said that the judgment delivered by the Court of Appeal in Abuja on Monday regarding the party’s congresses does not affect the emergence of its candidates through direct primaries. In a statement issued on Monday, by the ADC spokesman, Bolaji Abdullah

item.title: How Lyhanna’s murder case is shedding light on child abuse in France
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The rape and murder of 11-year-old Lyhanna exposed years of alleged police and judicial failures in France.

item.title: WAPCP Pushes Nationwide Adoption Of Consultant Pharmacist Cadre
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The West African Postgraduate College of Pharmacists (WAPCP), Nigeria Chapter, has called on the Federal Government, federal health institutions and state governments to fully implement the consultant pharmacist cadre across the country, describing the move as critical to improving patient care, med

item.title: Heatwave: Over 10,000 people died in Europe in June – Report
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: EuroMOMO does ⁠not publish excess deaths per individual country, but it noted that France and Belgium were the only two countries in Europe to log “very high excess” mortality in the last week of June. The post Heatwave: Over 10,000 people died in Europe in June – Report appeared first on Prem

item.title: ADC appeals A’Court ruling, says it’s legally unsustainable
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has assured members of the party and the general public that the judgment delivered by the Court of Appeal in Abuja on Monday has no effect whatsoever on the direct primaries through which the party’s candidates have emerged at all levels. Reacting to the

item.title: Yobe: Police arrest 63 suspected drug dealers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Yobe State Police Command has again arrested sixty-three suspected dealers in illicit drug trafficking, substance abuse and other criminal activities. The disclosure was made by the Command through its PPRO, SP Dungus Abdulkarim, in a press statement issued to journalists on Monday. The developm

item.title: Don’t lose sleep, Atiku tells ADC supporters over judgment on congresses
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has urged party members and millions of Nigerians yearning for genuine democratic change to remain calm, resolute and focused, stressing that contrary to the misleading narratives being aggressively circulated in some quarters, the Court of Appeal did not invalid

item.title: Dutch Referee Dies After Being Dropped From World Cup
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Dutch football referee Rob Dieperink has died weeks after he was dropped from officiating at the World Cup following a police investigation in the UK. Dieperink had been selected to be a video assistant referee (VAR) official at this summer’s tournament, but was removed from FIFA’s list
